perturbation.original_text: 2. Vendor agrees not to use, or permit others to use, the Proprietary Information, other than for the purpose of monitoring sales through distribution. Vendor agrees to make no more than five (5) copies of the Proprietary Information unless otherwise agreed in writing between the parties; and Vendor agrees to limit distribution of and access to the Proprietary Information to those of Vendor's personnel who require access to Proprietary Information for the foregoing purpose. Vendor agrees not to directly contact, for the purpose of soliciting, or selling Product directly to any customer or dealer listed in the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Vendor agrees to use the Proprietary Information only for high-level market analysis. Vendor may make up to ten (10) copies of the Proprietary Information. Vendor agrees to distribute and grant access to Proprietary Information only to key managerial personnel within the Vendor's organization. Vendor may contact customers or dealers listed in the Proprietary Information for feedback, so long as no direct sales are involved.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the use of Proprietary Information to monitoring sales, limits copies to five, restricts access to personnel requiring it for the specified purpose, and prohibits direct contact with listed customers/dealers. The modified text expands the use to market analysis, increases the copy limit to ten, broadens access to key managerial personnel, and allows contact with customers/dealers for feedback. This contradicts the original restrictions on use, distribution, and contact within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Vendor agrees to use the Proprietary Information only for high-level market analysis. Vendor may make up to ten (10) copies of the Proprietary Information. Vendor agrees to distribute and grant access to Proprietary Information only to key managerial personnel within the Vendor's organization. Vendor may contact customers or dealers listed in the Proprietary Information for feedback, so long as no direct sales are involved.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original restrictions on the use, distribution, and contact related to the Proprietary Information, as described in the explanation. The altered clause expands the scope of usage, increases copy limits, broadens access, and permits customer contact, which conflicts with the original text's limitations. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating the contradiction is within the same section. The original text restricts activity, while the altered text enables activity which causes a strong contradiction. Thus, it is a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be the property of Ingram, and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement or Ingram's request, be immediately returned to Ingram.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be jointly owned by Ingram and Vendor, and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement, Vendor will retain a copy for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that all copies of the Proprietary Information are the property of Ingram and must be returned upon expiration. The modified text indicates joint ownership and allows Vendor to retain a copy after expiration, which contradicts Ingram's sole ownership and right to immediate return. This in-text contradiction is on the ownership of the proprietary information, and on return obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that all copies of the Proprietary Information and all written descriptions, extractions, or summaries thereof, whether made by Vendor or Ingram, shall be jointly owned by Ingram and Vendor, and shall, upon expiration of this Agreement, Vendor will retain a copy for archival purposes.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that all copies of Proprietary Information belong solely to Ingram and must be returned upon expiration. The changed text introduces joint ownership between Ingram and Vendor and allows Vendor to retain a copy for archival purposes after expiration. The contradicted text restates that the Proprietary Information is jointly owned and Vendor can retain a copy. This creates a direct contradiction because the original agreement stipulates Ingram's sole ownership and complete return, while the modification and contradicted text grant joint ownership and retention rights to Vendor. This directly alters the ownership and return obligations, constituting a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that Ingram's public disclosure of the Proprietary Information, except pursuant to a confidential disclosure agreement, to any party will release Vendor from the obligation of confidentiality with respect to that portion of the Proprietary Information actually disclosed by Ingram.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that any disclosure of the Proprietary Information by Ingram, regardless of confidentiality agreements or the party it is disclosed to, shall not release Vendor from any obligations under this agreement. Vendor's confidentiality obligations remain in full effect irrespective of Ingram's actions.
perturbation.explanation: The original text releases Vendor from confidentiality obligations if Ingram publicly discloses the information. The modified text states that Vendor's confidentiality obligations remain, regardless of Ingram's actions, creating an in-text contradiction on whether public disclosure by Ingram affects Vendor's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Vendor and Ingram mutually agree that any disclosure of the Proprietary Information by Ingram, regardless of confidentiality agreements or the party it is disclosed to, shall not release Vendor from any obligations under this agreement. Vendor's confidentiality obligations remain in full effect irrespective of Ingram's actions.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Vendor is released from confidentiality obligations if Ingram publicly discloses Proprietary Information. The changed text states that Vendor's confidentiality obligations remain regardless of Ingram's actions, contradicting the original clause and therefore itself as the contradicted text is identical to the changed text. This creates a direct conflict regarding whether Ingram's disclosure affects Vendor's obligations, fulfilling the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.
